memory_context.summary: Recon origins=24, services=24, tech_stack=none, exploit_signals=14, rag_hits=11.
memory_context.persisted_summary: Recon origins=24, services=24, tech_stack=none, exploit_signals=14, rag_hits=11.

value.title: Jenkins Endpoint Exposure and Legacy CVEs
value.summary: Jenkins often exposes metadata endpoints; verify auth and known CVE coverage.
value.snippet: Audit login flow, crumbs API, and plugin version signals before exploit validation.

value.title: Legacy TLS Handshake and HTTPS Validation
value.summary: Legacy TLS protocol support should escalate into targeted HTTPS validation and hardening review.
value.snippet: When TLSv1/TLSv1.1 or weak transport traits are observed, feed the origin back into template-based HTTPS validation and passive hardening checks.

value.title: Memcached Public Exposure and Stats Leakage
value.summary: Public memcached responses often indicate broad unauthenticated exposure and version-bearing metadata.
value.snippet: Use bounded version and stats probes, then drive CVE lookup and safe sandbox validation from the discovered version.

value.title: Spring Boot Actuator and Env Exposure
value.summary: Spring deployments may expose actuator endpoints and configuration metadata.
value.snippet: Probe /actuator and common management endpoints. Validate safe endpoint exposure policy.

value.title: Grafana Auth and Plugin Exposure Checks
value.summary: Grafana deployments often expose login endpoints and plugin metadata that influence exploitability.
value.snippet: Validate auth hardening, plugin list exposure, and known CVE coverage for detected Grafana versions.

rag_intel_hits.title: Jenkins Endpoint Exposure and Legacy CVEs
Legacy TLS Handshake and HTTPS Validation
Memcached Public Exposure and Stats Leakage
Spring Boot Actuator and Env Exposure
Grafana Auth and Plugin Exposure Checks
